name: Dogs Refuse Food / Won't Face South
payoffWindow: Session 1 Act III (Lake's camp confirms animal instinct was correct)
content: The sledge dogs exhibit unexplained behavioral changes: refusing food, showing agitation, refusing to face south. The dog handler interprets this as weather sensitivity. In truth, the animals sense what humans rationalize away — Stratum II bleeding into Stratum I. This is the earliest signal that the rational frame is insufficient. When the expedition reaches Lake's camp and finds the star-shaped graves and the evidence of what happened to the first expedition's dogs, the players can look back and realize the dogs knew all along.
notes: Earliest signal that Stratum I is insufficient. Animals know before humans. Lovecraft's dogs savage toward specimens, hate the soapstone.
